title: Sidebar artifact tree selection and filtering utilities
purpose: Provide pure selector functions to filter and transform artifact data for sidebar tree display, including pinned nodes, blueprints, snippets, and search filtering.

steps.content: Filter the input items to include only those with pinned property set to true.
Return the filtered array of pinned items.

methods.name: selectBlueprintNodes
methods.normalized_params: <T extends { id: string; blueprint?: boolean; deprecated?: boolean; name: string; lastModified: number }>(documents: T[]): Array<T & { type: 'document' }>

steps.content: Filter the documents to include only those marked as blueprints and not ending with 'vibeinstructions'.
Create a shallow copy of the filtered documents.
Sort the copied documents by lastModified in descending order.
Map each document to include a type property set to 'document'.
Return the transformed and sorted array of blueprint documents.

methods.name: selectLinkedSnippets
methods.normalized_params: <T extends { name: string; content?: string; lastModified: number; linked?: boolean; filePath?: string; dirty?: boolean }>(snippets: T[]): Array<T & { type: 'snippet'; _filePath: string; _dirty: boolean }>

steps.content: Filter the snippets to include only those marked as linked or containing linked property in their parsed content.
Create a shallow copy of the filtered snippets.
Sort the copied snippets by lastModified in descending order.
Map each snippet to parse its content and extract file path and dirty status.
Update the snippet name based on parsed file path or original name.
Return the transformed and sorted array of linked snippets with additional metadata.
Map each diagram in the input to include a type property set to 'diagram'.
Return the transformed array of diagrams.

methods.name: selectCatchAllDocuments
methods.normalized_params: <T extends { name: string; blueprint?: boolean }>(documents: T[]): Array<T & { type: 'document' }>

steps.content: Filter the documents to exclude those ending with 'vibeinstructions', marked as blueprints, or named 'task-graph'.
Map each filtered document to include a type property set to 'document'.
Return the transformed array of catch-all documents.

methods.name: selectCatchAllDesigns
methods.normalized_params: <T extends { content?: string; lastModified?: number }>(designs: T[]): Array<T & { content: string; lastModified: number; type: 'design' }>

steps.content: Map each design in the input to provide default values for content and lastModified if they are missing.
Include a type property set to 'design' in each mapped design.
Return the transformed array of designs with default values.

methods.name: selectCatchAllSpreadsheets
methods.normalized_params: <T extends { content?: string; lastModified?: number }>(spreadsheets: T[]): Array<T & { content: string; lastModified: number; type: 'spreadsheet' }>

steps.content: Map each spreadsheet in the input to provide default values for content and lastModified if they are missing.
Include a type property set to 'spreadsheet' in each mapped spreadsheet.
Return the transformed array of spreadsheets with default values.

methods.name: selectCatchAllSnippets
methods.normalized_params: <T extends { name: string; content?: string; lastModified: number; linked?: boolean }>(snippets: T[]): Array<T & { type: 'snippet' }>

steps.content: Create a set to track seen group IDs for deduplication.
Create a shallow copy of the input snippets.
Sort the copied snippets by lastModified in descending order.
Filter snippets to exclude those ending with 'vibeinstructions', marked as linked, or containing linked property in parsed content.
For snippets with group IDs, ensure they are not duplicates by checking against the seen groups set.
Map each filtered snippet to extract and update its name based on parsed group name or original name.
Return the transformed and deduplicated array of catch-all snippets.

methods.name: filterTreeBySearch
methods.normalized_params: (sections: TreeSection[], query: string): { visibleNodes: Set<string>; sectionsWithMatches: Set<string> }

steps.content: Trim the search query and check if it is empty.
IF the query is empty, mark all nodes and sections as visible.
ELSE convert the trimmed query to lowercase for case-insensitive matching.
For each section, check if any of its leaves match the query.
IF a leaf matches, add its ID to the visible nodes set.
IF a section contains at least one matching leaf, add its ID to the sections with matches set.
Return the sets of visible nodes and sections with matches.
